assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly identifies PodDisruptionBudgets as 'Most Common' culprit and states 'Based on your symptoms, you probably have PDBs that are too restrictive'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Multiple PDB check commands provided including 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' and variations with additional flags

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes commands like 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase=Running -o wide | grep <old-node-names>' and event checking commands

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: No mention of resource constraints, capacity issues, or pending pods as potential causes of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Multiple PDB patch commands provided including 'kubectl patch pdb <pdb-name> -n <namespace> --type merge -p '{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}' and similar variations

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: No mention of GKE's PDB timeout mechanism or automatic notifications when eviction is blocked

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: No mention of surge upgrade settings or surge configuration as a potential solution

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: No mention of admission controller webhooks or webhook issues as potential causes of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Includes monitoring commands like 'watch -n 30 kubectl get nodes -o wide' and event monitoring to track progress

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: No mention of specific GKE disruption-event notifications, POD_PDB_VIOLATION events, or Cloud Logging/Pub/Sub integration
